title: Cyber-Sovereign Execution Moat
skills: Map assets, dependencies, owners, controls, SBOM/CBOM, and criticality.
Prioritize weaknesses by exploitability, blast radius, business impact, and remediation confidence.
Create bounded patches, policy changes, config changes, and compensating controls.
Validate remediation without breaking functionality or policy.
Package claim, baseline, artifacts, tests, ledgers, reviewer attestations, rollback path, and replay path.
Stress-test the proof, hunt for reward hacks, validate negative controls, and record bounded challenges.
Coordinate budgets, bonds, proof submission, review, credentials, reputation, and settlement.
Grant scoped upgrade rights only after evidence, validation, risk control, and rollback readiness.
plans: 30/60/90-day portfolio across identity, supply chain, access, deployment gates, and rollback.
One controlled defensive cyber remediation loop with public-safe Evidence Docket.
Unit/security tests, static analysis, SBOM/VEX-style evidence, reviewer attestation, red-team challenge, delayed monitoring.
Route verified value into stronger agents, better harnesses, deeper asset graphs, higher-value missions, and safer rollout paths.
goals: Prove that a remediation lowers risk under authorized tests and reviewer scrutiny.
Accelerate security/procurement review without weakening controls.
Ensure accepted improvements leave proof future missions can reuse.
Convert security lessons into reusable capability across teams, suppliers, and workflows.
Reinvest verified work into stronger agents, tools, audits, compute, science infrastructure, and energy-relevant experimentation without claiming present superintelligence.
agents: scope, authority, risk appetite, claim boundary
public-safe threat context and exposure mapping
